steps.description: Prepare Te precursor solution by mixing Te powder with TBP (or TOP).
Cool down the Te-TBP (or Te-TOP) complex to room temperature.
Dissolve BiCl3 in ethylene glycol to prepare the Bi precursor solution.
Add 1 mL of thioglycolic acid (TGA) as a directing agent to the dissolved Bi precursor solution.
Sonicate the Bi-EG-TGA mixture to obtain a homogeneous solution.
Mix the Te-TOP (or Te-TBP) complex solution with the Bi-EG solution and transfer to a 100 mL Teflon vessel.
Run the mixture in the MW reactor for 2 min dwell time at 220°C under constant stirring to form Bi2Te3 nanoplatelets.

equipment.name: microwave reactor
equipment.settings: flexiWAVE, 1800 W, multivessel high pressure rotor

steps.description: Cool the product (dark powder) down to room temperature.
Separate particles from the reaction mixture by centrifuging.
Wash the separated particles with iso-propanol and acetone several times.

equipment.name: microwave reactor (flexiWAVE)
equipment.settings: 1800 W, multivessel high pressure rotor, 100 mL Teflon vessels

synthesis.notes: The synthesis yields approximately 2 g per vessel (>8 g per batch using 4 parallel vessels). TBP or TOP can be used as the Te complexing agent. All chemicals were used as received without further purification. The mechanism involves reduction of Bi3+ by ethylene glycol to metallic Bi, which then reacts with Te(TOP) to form crystalline Bi2Te3. The as-prepared Bi2Te3 phases were clearly separated from the EG solvent.

steps.description: Prepare Te precursor solution by heating a mixture of Te powder in TBP (or TOP) under MW field for about 90 s at 220°C under constant stirring, then left to cool down.
Dissolve BiCl3 and equivalent moles of SbCl3 (to achieve Bi1.8Sb0.2Te3 stoichiometry) in ethylene glycol. Add 1 mL TGA as a directing agent, then sonicate to obtain a homogeneous solution.
Mix the Te-TOP (or Te-TBP) complex solution and the Bi/Sb-EG solution together and add to a 100 mL Teflon vessel.
Run the mixture in the MW reactor for 2 min dwell time at 220°C under constant stirring. Product is a dark powder (~2 g per vessel).

equipment.name: microwave reactor
equipment.settings: flexiWAVE, 1800 W, multivessel high pressure rotor, 220°C, 2 min dwell time, constant stirring

steps.description: Cool the product down to room temperature.
Separate particles from the reaction mixture by centrifuging and washing with iso-propanol and acetone several times.

equipment.name: microwave reactor (flexiWAVE)
equipment.settings: 1800 W, multivessel high pressure rotor, 100 mL Teflon vessels

synthesis.notes: The stoichiometry of ternary compositions is tuned by replacing part of the BiCl3 precursor with equivalent moles of SbCl3. For Bi1.8Sb0.2Te3, a small fraction of BiCl3 is replaced by SbCl3. The synthesis yields about 2 g per vessel; using 4 parallel vessels yields >8 g per batch. All chemicals were used as received without further purification.

steps.description: Prepare Te precursor solution by heating a mixture of Te in TBP (or TOP) under MW field for about 90 s at 220°C under constant stirring, then left to cool down.

Dissolve BiCl3 and SbCl3 (in stoichiometric ratio for Bi0.5Sb1.5Te3, replacing equivalent moles of BiCl3 with SbCl3) in ethylene glycol. Add 1 mL TGA as a directing agent, then sonicate to obtain a homogeneous solution.
Mix the Te-TOP (or Te-TBP) complex solution and the Bi/Sb-EG solution and add to a 100 mL Teflon vessel.
Run the mixture in the MW reactor for 2 min dwell time at 220°C under constant stirring. Product is a dark powder (~2 g per vessel).

equipment.name: microwave reactor
equipment.settings: flexiWAVE, 1800 W, multivessel high pressure rotor, 220°C, 2 min dwell, constant stirring

steps.description: Cool the product down to room temperature.
Separate particles from the reaction mixture by centrifuging and washing with iso-propanol and acetone several times.

Consolidate the nanopowders using spark plasma sintering (SPS) with 15 mm inner diameter graphite cylinders. SPS temperature set to 400°C with a heating rate of 30°C/min and 1-min dwell time. 50 MPa pressure applied gradually. Holding time set to 1 min for both applied current and pressure. Pellet cooled from 400°C to 250°C while load decreased from 50 MPa to 0 MPa.

equipment.name: spark plasma sintering (SPS) system
equipment.settings: 400°C, 30°C/min heating rate, 1 min dwell, 50 MPa pressure, graphite die 15 mm inner diameter

steps.description: Polish the pellets in three steps to remove graphite contamination and obtain smooth surface with 1 µm roughness.

equipment.name: microwave reactor (flexiWAVE)
equipment.settings: 1800 W, multivessel high pressure rotor, up to 15 x 100 mL Teflon vessels

synthesis.notes: The stoichiometry of ternary Bi0.5Sb1.5Te3 is tuned by replacing the appropriate moles of BiCl3 with SbCl3 in the precursor solution. The synthesis yields ~2 g per vessel; using 4 vessels in parallel gives >8 g per batch. The reaction mechanism involves reduction of Bi3+/Sb3+ by ethylene glycol to metallic Bi/Sb, which then reacts with Te(TOP) to form the crystalline Bi2-xSbxTe3 phase. Bi0.5Sb1.5Te3 is a p-type thermoelectric material with ZT reaching ~0.76 at 523 K.

steps.description: Prepare Te precursor solution by heating a mixture of Te powder in TBP (or TOP) under MW field for about 90 s at 220°C under constant stirring, then left to cool down.
Dissolve SbCl3 in ethylene glycol to prepare the Sb precursor solution. For Sb2Te3, SbCl3 replaces BiCl3 with equivalent moles.
Add 1 mL aliquot of TGA as a directing agent to the dissolved Sb precursor, then sonicate to obtain a homogeneous solution.
Mix the Te-TOP (or Te-TBP) complex solution and the Sb-EG solution together and add to a 100 mL Teflon vessel.
Run the mixture in the MW reactor for 2 min dwell time at 220°C under constant stirring. Product is a dark powder (~2 g per vessel).

equipment.name: microwave reactor
equipment.settings: flexiWAVE, 1800 W, multivessel high pressure rotor, 220°C, 2 min dwell, constant stirring

steps.description: Cool the product down to room temperature.
Separate particles from the reaction mixture by centrifuging and washing with iso-propanol and acetone several times.

equipment.name: microwave reactor (flexiWAVE)
equipment.settings: 1800 W, multivessel high pressure rotor, 100 mL Teflon vessels

synthesis.notes: For Sb2Te3, SbCl3 fully replaces BiCl3 with equivalent moles. The synthesis yields ~2 g per vessel and >8 g per batch using 4 parallel reaction vessels. The reaction mechanism involves reduction of Sb3+ ions to metallic Sb by EG, which then reacts with Te(TOP) to form Sb2Te3. The highest ZT of 1.37 was achieved at 523 K after SPS consolidation at 400°C, 50 MPa, 1 min dwell.

steps.description: Preparation of Te precursor solution as a complex with TBP under microwave field.
Dissolving SbCl3 in ethylene glycol.
Adding TGA as a directing agent and sonicating to obtain a homogeneous solution.
Mixing the Te-TBP complex and Sb-EG solutions in a Teflon vessel.
Microwave-assisted thermolysis reaction.
Separating particles from the reaction mixture by centrifuging and washing.

synthesis.notes: The synthesis yields about 2 g per vessel, with a total yield of >8 g per batch using 4 vessels. Sb2Te3 exhibits p-type character.

steps.description: Preparation of Te precursor solution by heating Te powder in TBP under microwave field.
Dissolving BiCl3 and SbCl3 in ethylene glycol to match the stoichiometry of Bi0.5Sb1.5Te3.
Adding thioglycolic acid (TGA) as a directing agent and sonicating to obtain a homogeneous solution.
Mixing the Te-TBP complex and the Bi/Sb-EG solution in a Teflon vessel.
Microwave-assisted thermolysis of the mixture.
Separating the product from the solvent and washing with iso-propanol and acetone.

synthesis.notes: The stoichiometry was tuned by replacing BiCl3 with equivalent moles of SbCl3 to achieve the Bi0.5Sb1.5Te3 composition. Yield is approximately 2g per vessel.

steps.description: Preparation of Te precursor solution as a complex with TBP or TOP under microwave field.
Dissolving BiCl3 (or equivalent moles of SbCl3 for ternary compositions) in ethylene glycol.
Adding TGA as a directing agent to the Bi/Sb solution and sonicating to obtain a homogeneous solution.
Mixing the Te-TOP/TBP complex and Bi/Sb-EG solutions in a Teflon vessel.
Microwave-assisted thermolysis reaction.
Separating particles from the reaction mixture and washing.

synthesis.notes: The stoichiometry is tuned by replacing BiCl3 with equivalent moles of SbCl3. The method yields >8g per batch using 4 parallel vessels.

steps.description: Preparation of Te precursor solution. Heat mixture of Te powder in TBP (or TOP) under MW field.
Preparation of Bi/Sb precursor. Dissolve BiCl3 and SbCl3 in ethylene glycol. For Bi1.8Sb0.2Te3, replace portion of BiCl3 with equivalent moles of SbCl3.
Add TGA to dissolved precursors and sonicate. Mix Te-TOP complex solution and Bi/Sb-EG solution.
MW-assisted thermolysis reaction. Add mixture to 100 mL Teflon vessel and run in MW reactor.
Cool to room temperature. Separate product from solvent by centrifuging and wash with iso-propanol and acetone several times.
Consolidation of nanopowders using Spark Plasma Sintering (SPS).

synthesis.notes: The stoichiometry of ternary compositions is tuned by replacing BiCl3 precursor with equivalent moles of SbCl3. For Bi1.8Sb0.2Te3, the ratio of Bi:Sb is 9:1. The synthesis yields >8 g per batch using 4 parallel reaction vessels. SPS heating rate was 30°C/min. Pellets were cooled from 400°C to 250°C while load decreased from 50 MPa to 0 MPa.

steps.description: Preparation of Te precursor solution. Te powder is complexed with TBP (or TOP) by heating under MW field.
Preparation of Bi/Sb precursor solution. BiCl3 (or SbCl3) is dissolved in ethylene glycol. For Bi0.5Sb1.5Te3, BiCl3 is replaced with equivalent moles of SbCl3 to achieve 1:3 Bi:Sb molar ratio.
Add TGA to the dissolved Bi/Sb precursors and sonicate to obtain a homogeneous solution. Then mix the Te-TOP/TBP complex solution with the Bi/Sb-EG solution.
Microwave-assisted thermolysis reaction. The mixed solution is run in the MW reactor.

equipment.name: Microwave reactor
equipment.settings: flexiWAVE, 1800 Watt, multivessel high pressure rotor

steps.description: Cool down to room temperature. Separate particles from solvent by centrifuging and washing with iso-propanol and acetone several times.
Consolidation of nanopowders into pellets using Spark Plasma Sintering (SPS).

synthesis.notes: The stoichiometry of ternary compositions is tuned by replacing BiCl3 precursor with equivalent moles of SbCl3. For Bi0.5Sb1.5Te3, the Bi:Sb molar ratio is 1:3. steps.description: Preparation of Te precursor solution by heating Te powder in TBP or TOP under microwave field.
Preparation of Sb precursor by dissolving SbCl3 in ethylene glycol. Add TGA and sonicate to obtain homogeneous solution.
Mix the prepared Te-TOP/TBP complex solution and Sb-EG solution.
Microwave-assisted reaction in Teflon vessel.
Cool down the product to room temperature.
Separate particles from solvent by centrifuging and wash with iso-propanol and acetone several times.

steps.description: Prepare Te precursor solution by heating a mixture of Te powder and TOP under microwave field.
Dissolve BiCl3 in ethylene glycol to prepare Bi precursor solution.
Add thioglycolic acid (TGA) to the dissolved Bi precursor solution and sonicate to obtain a homogeneous solution.
Mix the prepared Te-TOP complex solution and the Bi-EG solution in a Teflon vessel.
Run the mixture in the microwave reactor for reaction.

equipment.name: microwave reactor
equipment.settings: flexiWAVE, 1800 Watt, multivessel high pressure rotor

steps.description: Cool down the product to room temperature.
Separate particles from the reaction mixture by centrifuging and washing with iso-propanol and acetone several times.

equipment.name: microwave reactor
equipment.settings: flexiWAVE, 1800 Watt, equipped with multivessel high pressure rotor

synthesis.notes: Synthesis yields about 2 g per vessel. The multivessel high pressure rotor allows parallel synthesis in 4 vessels per run, yielding >8 g total. The method is described as a microwave-assisted polyol synthesis.

steps.description: Prepare Te precursor solution by heating a mixture of Te powder in TBP (or TOP) under microwave field.
Dissolve BiCl3 (and SbCl3 for ternary compositions) in ethylene glycol to prepare Bi/Sb precursor solution.
Add thioglycolic acid to the dissolved Bi/Sb precursor solution and sonicate to obtain a homogeneous solution.
Mix the prepared Te-TOP complex solution and the Bi/Sb-EG solution.
Run the mixed solution in a microwave reactor for 2 min at 220°C under constant stirring.

equipment.name: microwave reactor
equipment.settings: flexiWAVE-Milestone, 1800 Watt; equipped with multivessel high pressure rotor

steps.description: Cool down the product to room temperature.
Separate particles from the reaction mixture by centrifuging and washing with iso-propanol and acetone several times.

equipment.name: microwave reactor
equipment.settings: flexiWAVE-Milestone, 1800 Watt; equipped with multivessel high pressure rotor

synthesis.notes: The stoichiometry of ternary compositions can easily be tuned by replacing the BiCl3 precursor with equivalent moles of SbCl3. The multivessel high pressure rotor was used for the MW synthesis work, where only four of the fifteen reactor positions were more than enough to prepare large quantity (>8 g) of the desired materials in a single run.

steps.description: Prepare Te precursor solution by heating a mixture of Te powder with TBP (or TOP) under microwave field.
Dissolve BiCl3 and SbCl3 in ethylene glycol to prepare Bi/Sb precursor solution. For ternary compositions like Bi0.5Sb1.5Te3, replace BiCl3 with equivalent moles of SbCl3.
Add thioglycolic acid (TGA) as a directing agent to the dissolved Bi/Sb precursor solution and sonicate to obtain a homogeneous solution.
Mix the prepared Te-TOP complex solution and the Bi/Sb-EG solution, then add to a 100 mL Teflon vessel.
Run the mixture in the microwave reactor for 2 minutes at 220°C under constant stirring to form Bi0.5Sb1.5Te3 nanoplatelets.

equipment.name: microwave reactor
equipment.settings: flexiWAVE, 1800 Watt, multivessel high pressure rotor

steps.description: Cool down the product to room temperature.
Separate the dark powder product from the solvent by centrifuging and washing with iso-propanol and acetone several times.

equipment.name: microwave reactor
equipment.settings: flexiWAVE, 1800 Watt, equipped with multivessel high pressure rotor

synthesis.notes: The synthesis yields about 2 g per vessel, and using four vessels in a single run can produce >8 g of material. The stoichiometry of ternary compositions is tuned by replacing BiCl3 with equivalent moles of SbCl3. The method is rapid, energy-effective, and scalable.

steps.description: Prepare Te precursor solution by heating a mixture of Te powder and TBP (or TOP) under microwave field.
Dissolve SbCl3 in ethylene glycol to prepare Sb precursor solution.
Add thioglycolic acid (TGA) to the dissolved Sb precursor solution and sonicate to obtain a homogeneous solution.
Mix the prepared Te-TOP complex and Sb-EG solutions and add to a 100 mL Teflon vessel.
Run the mixture in the microwave reactor for 2 minutes at 220°C under constant stirring.

equipment.name: microwave reactor
equipment.settings: flexiWAVE, 1800 Watt, multivessel high pressure rotor

steps.description: Cool down the product (dark powder) to room temperature.
Separate particles from the reaction mixture by centrifuging and washing with iso-propanol and acetone several times.

equipment.name: microwave reactor
equipment.settings: flexiWAVE, 1800 Watt, equipped with multivessel high pressure rotor
